seo.title: 100 Web Analytics resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Web Analytics resources for engineers.

content.intro: Modern web analytics for developers has shifted from generic pageview tracking to privacy-first, event-driven architectures. This resource provides a curated list of tools and implementation strategies for developers looking to balance data utility with user privacy and regulatory compliance.

items.title: Plausible Analytics
items.description: Open-source, lightweight (<1KB) alternative to Google Analytics. It avoids cookies and is fully GDPR, CCPA, and PECR compliant by default.

items.title: Fathom Analytics
items.description: Privacy-centric tool focusing on high-traffic performance. It uses a custom domain feature to bypass ad-blockers effectively.

items.title: Simple Analytics
items.description: A clean, no-cookie analytics tool that provides a 'bypass ad-blockers' script and a simple JSON API for data export.

items.title: Pirsch Analytics
items.description: A powerful, server-side focused analytics tool that integrates directly via SDKs for Go, PHP, and Python, or a standard JS snippet.

items.title: Cloudflare Web Analytics
items.description: Privacy-first analytics that doesn't use any client-side state like cookies or localStorage. Free for all Cloudflare users.

items.title: Microanalytics.io
items.description: A minimalist analytics tool built in the EU. Focuses on speed and zero tracking of personal data.

items.title: Panelbear
items.description: Real-time analytics with a focus on performance monitoring and automated alerts for traffic spikes or drops.

items.title: Tinybird
items.description: An analytics platform for developers to build real-time data products at scale using SQL on top of event streams.

items.title: Wide Angle Analytics
items.description: Strictly GDPR-compliant B2B analytics with support for custom event tracking and complex domain filtering.

items.title: GoatCounter
items.description: Open-source web analytics platform available as a hosted service or self-hosted binary. No tracking of personal data.

items.title: Umami
items.description: A simple, fast, privacy-focused alternative to Google Analytics. Written in Node.js and uses Prisma for database flexibility (PostgreSQL/MySQL).

items.title: Matomo (On-Premise)
items.description: The most feature-complete open-source analytics platform. Provides full data ownership and extensive plugin ecosystem.

items.title: Ackee
items.description: Node.js based analytics tool that provides a GraphQL API to interact with your data. Ideal for headless CMS setups.

items.title: PostHog (Self-Hosted)
items.description: An all-in-one product suite including event tracking, session recording, and feature flags. Best deployed via Docker or Kubernetes.

items.title: Shynet
items.description: A modern, privacy-friendly, and detailed web analytics tool that doesn't use cookies or JS tracking pixels (it uses a 1x1 GIF).

items.title: Offen Fair Web Analytics
items.description: An open-source analytics tool that treats users as equal partners by giving them access to their own data.

items.title: ClickHouse
items.description: The column-oriented DBMS used by Plausible and PostHog. Essential for developers building custom high-scale analytics engines.

items.title: Snowplow Open Source
items.description: Enterprise-grade event data collection platform. Allows for complex pipeline customization and data lake integration.

items.title: RudderStack
items.description: Open-source Customer Data Platform (CDP). Use it to route event data from your app to multiple analytics destinations.

items.title: Metabase
items.description: Connect Metabase to your self-hosted analytics database (PostgreSQL/ClickHouse) to create custom dashboards and BI reports.

items.title: Mixpanel JQL
items.description: Use Javascript Query Language (JQL) within Mixpanel to perform complex analysis on raw event data that standard UIs cannot handle.

items.title: Amplitude Browser SDK
items.description: Implement robust product analytics with built-in support for identity management and cross-device tracking.

items.title: Vercel Web Analytics
items.description: Zero-config analytics for Next.js applications. It tracks core web vitals and basic traffic metrics automatically.

items.title: Segment Analytics.js
items.description: The industry standard for decoupling your application code from specific analytics vendors. Write once, route everywhere.

items.title: GA4 Measurement Protocol
items.description: Bypass client-side limitations by sending event data directly from your server to Google Analytics 4 via REST API.

items.title: PostHog Feature Flags
items.description: Integrate analytics with deployment. Track how specific features impact user behavior using built-in flags and cohorts.

items.title: Supabase pg_analytics
items.description: Leverage Postgres-native analytics extensions to track user interactions directly within your application database.

items.title: Proxying Scripts with Next.js Rewrites
items.description: Configure 'next.config.js' to proxy analytics scripts through your own domain to improve data accuracy and bypass blockers.

items.title: A/B Testing with Middleware
items.description: Use Edge Middleware (Vercel/Cloudflare) to bucket users and send variant IDs to your analytics provider without layout shift.

items.title: Custom Event Schema Validation
items.description: Use JSON Schema or TypeScript interfaces to validate event payloads before sending them to your analytics pipeline.

pro_tips: Serve analytics scripts from a sub-path of your main domain (e.g., /stats/script.js) using a reverse proxy to minimize the impact of ad-blockers.
Always use server-side event tracking for critical business logic, such as successful payments or API credit usage, to ensure 100% data accuracy.
Anonymize IP addresses by masking the last octet before storage to comply with GDPR 'Privacy by Design' principles without losing geographic trends.
Implement 'Heartbeat' events for long-running sessions or AI-chat interfaces to accurately measure engagement time beyond simple page exit events.
Audit your analytics payload regularly using browser developer tools to ensure no PII (Personally Identifiable Information) is accidentally leaked in URLs or properties.
